per_event_results.nary_reason: Multiple instigators (Walder Frey, Lame Lothar, Tywin Lannister) who did not personally execute; multiple executors (Roose Bolton, Ryman Frey, Hosteen Frey, Raymund Frey, Black Walder Frey, Frey crossbowmen, Bolton men-at-arms); multiple victims (Robb Stark, Catelyn Stark, Dacey Mormont, and numerous northmen); instigators are distinct from executors; canonical named set-piece referenced across the narrative.

Instigator (Littlefinger) is distinct from physical executor (Olenna); multiple unwitting instruments (Dontos, Sansa) in the delivery chain; single victim (Joffrey); named historical set-piece referenced throughout the series.

Instigator (Stannis, whose life force created the shadow) is distinct from executor (Melisandre, who performed the shadowbinding ritual) and victim (Renly); three distinct roles on the same event node.
Pre-declared clean dyad: Jaime Lannister kills Aerys II Targaryen. Single agent + single victim, no instigator or ordering third party. Per D8 gate: do not reify.

Pre-declared clean dyad: Tyrion Lannister kills Tywin Lannister with a crossbow. Single agent + single victim, acting alone. Per D8 gate: do not reify. (Also: Tyrion strangles Shae is a separate clean dyad, same rule.)

Multiple instigators (Cersei, Joffrey) are distinct from executors (Littlefinger, Lannister soldiers/City Watch); multiple victims (Ned arrested, guardsmen killed, Syrio Forel killed); and this is a named set-piece referenced across multiple narrative threads.

Instigator (Joffrey) is distinct from executor (Ser Ilyn Payne), making this a three-party event with a commanding party, an executing party, and a victim; additionally a named artifact (Ice) was wielded.

Multiple victims (Pycelle and Kevan Lannister), multiple agents (Varys plus his little-bird child agents who physically killed Pycelle), and an instigator-executor split (Aegon Targaryen is the political beneficiary Varys explicitly acts to serve, while Varys is the direct executor).

Multiple agents (Unsullied led by Grey Worm, three dragons), instigator (Daenerys) differs from direct executors, multiple victims (Good Masters broadly, Kraznys specifically), and this is a named set-piece battle referenced throughout the series.

Multiple executors (Jorah, Grey Worm, Daario) acting under a separate commander (Daenerys) who did not personally fight; multiple distinct forces (Unsullied, Dothraki, Stormcrows); a defending party (Mero/Second Sons) on the receiving end; and a named city as the target location — far exceeding a clean dyad.

Named set-piece anchor for downstream graph edges (Karstark army departure, weakening of Robb's host); requires LOCATED_AT edge to Riverrun; while Robb is both orderer and executor (no separate COMMANDS_IN), reification is warranted to capture the location participant and serve as a reference node for consequent events.

Named set-piece battle with multiple direct executors (Tyrion, Davos, Loras, Mandon Moore), multiple commanders on both sides (Stannis, Tywin, Cersei, Mace Tyrell), a contested city as location, and numerous other events/edges that reference this hub.
